title: Negative refcount on a MemoryError under OOM (over-decref) — _Py_NegativeRefcount via a list/subtype/tuple dealloc cascade
description: Under OOM a MemoryError is decref'd one time too many; the negative refcount is detected later by _Py_NegativeRefcount (Objects/object.c:275) when the object is freed again during an unrelated dealloc cascade (list_dealloc -> subtype_dealloc -> tuple_dealloc), aborting on debug builds. The dealloc cascade is the incidental detection site, not the defect.
notes: Corrupted object is a MemoryError (from the fatal's 'object type name'). Root cause = an unbalanced Py_DECREF of a MemoryError on an OOM error path (reachable via _pyrepl.utils fuzzing: gen_colors_from_token_stream/iter_display_chars/_ascii_control_repr/...); the _Py_NegativeRefcount detector fires at an incidental later decref (tuple item freed in a list->subtype->tuple teardown), so the dealloc-cascade chain is NOT the defect site and may vary across vehicles. Distinct from OOM-0005 (frame locals, PyStackRef_XCLOSE) and OOM-0019 (pegen parser, Py_XDECREF) -- same negative-refcount family, different subsystem; may share a root (unbalanced decref under OOM). Debug-only abort (Py_DEBUG-gated detector; silent UAF risk on release). Reproduces deterministically on ft_debug_asan/jit (same chain 2x). Minimization: PARTIAL/vehicle-only (over-decref site unisolated). Flagged oomNEW by fusil in-loop dedup; host ran a slightly stale fusil. Found 2026-06-19 from a fresh host download.
